info.name: WoodWing Assets
info.description: A Postman collection for working with WoodWing Assets, covering both the legacy /services API and the newer REST (/api) API, plus example environment variables for authentication.

item.name: Login and Logout
Standard Login and Logout

request.description: Authenticates against Assets Server with {{username}} and {{password}} and starts a session. On success, the Tests script automatically stores the csrfToken from the response body as {{Current X-CRSF-TOKEN}} and the authToken cookie (set via Set-Cookie) as {{authToken}} — no manual copying needed.

exec: // /services/login returns the CSRF token in the JSON body.
// The auth cookie is set via a Set-Cookie header, not the body.
// Postman's cookie jar picks it up automatically, so read it back here.

request.description: Ends the session created by Login. Requires the session cookie and the X-CSRF-TOKEN header obtained at login. On completion, the Tests script clears {{authToken}} and {{Current X-CRSF-TOKEN}} from the environment.

item.description: Session-based login and logout using a regular username/password credential pair. This flow uses both a session cookie and an X-CSRF-TOKEN header.

item.name: API Login and Logout

exec: // apilogin returns authToken directly in the JSON body (unlike /services/login,
// which sets it via a Set-Cookie header instead).

formdata.description: Enter your user name for your API User
Enter your password for your API User
Name of client logging in with API User

request.description: Use this login call when a dedicated API user is available (as opposed to a regular interactive user). Authenticates with {{api_user_name}} / {{api_password}}. Unlike the standard Login call, apilogin returns the authToken directly in the JSON body rather than as a cookie; the Tests script automatically stores it as {{authToken}}. This flow does not require a CSRF token.

Ends the session created by Login using 'apilogin'. This flow authenticates via the session cookie only — no CSRF token is required. On completion, the Tests script clears {{authToken}} from the environment.

item.description: Login and logout using a dedicated API user (api_user_name/api_password). This flow authenticates via the session cookie only; no CSRF token is required.
Authentication calls for Assets Server: the standard session-based login/logout flow (cookie + CSRF token), and the API-key-based apilogin/logout flow (cookie only, no CSRF).

item.name: Metadata Reports
Metadata report call asking for JSON and using a query to get all assets with status of Production

request.description: Generates a metadata report in JSON for all assets matching the query `status:Production`. The `report` segment in the URL path is just the output filename, not a saved report definition, and can be renamed freely.

item.name: Metadata report call asking for CSV and using a query to get all assets with status of Production

query.description: Query for repor that asks for all assets whose status is 'production'

request.description: Same report as the JSON version above, returned as CSV — useful for opening directly in Excel.

item.name: Metadata report call asking for JSON and using the assetPath parameter

query.description: This is the path to the Assets you want to report on

request.description: Generates a metadata report in JSON for all assets under the folder given in the `assetPath` parameter, instead of a query.

item.description: Generates downloadable metadata reports (JSON or CSV) for assets matching a query or folder path, using the legacy /metadata/{name}.{format} endpoint.

item.name: Operations
Search and Browse

query.description: assetPath
folderPath
true/false
true/false
Comma-delimited list of extensions

request.description: Lists the folders and/or assets directly under a given folder `path` (hierarchical browsing, similar to a file system directory listing). Use `includeFolders`/`includeAssets`/`includeExtensions` to control what's returned, and `fromRoot` to browse from the root instead of a specific path.

query.description: Required. Query asking for Assets with the value of 'apollo'. Lots of ways to query. Start here for docs: https://helpcenter.woodwing.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041851432
Optional First hit to be returned. Starting at 0 for the first hit. Used for paging
Optional. Number of hits to return. Default is 50. 0 returns no hits.
Optional. The sort order of returned hits. Comma-delimited list of fields to sort on.
Optional. For all fields use 'metadataToReturn=all', Leave null for only minimal fields. Default returns all fields.
Optional. Comma-delimited list fiels to return facets for. Ex: facets=tags,assetDomain
Optional. Replace <field> with the appropriate field you want. Ex: facet.assetDomain.selection=image,video
Optional. Default is false. Use when retrieving thumb, preview or original URL's and you won't need to authenticate against the server again.
Optional. Default is true. Any found text is highlighted.
Optional. Default for 6.75 and higher is false, 6.74 and lower true.  Collections returned in the results have an additional array with up to 4 thumbnailHits
Optional. Default is true. When set to true, an entry for this search is written to the Stats index.

request.description: Full-text and metadata search across all assets using Assets Server's query syntax (here searching for `tree`). Returns matching assets with metadata and ready-to-use thumbnail/preview/original URLs.

item.description: Locating assets: hierarchical folder/tree browsing (Browse) and free-text/metadata search (Search).

item.name: Working with assets

header.description: Use the X-CSRF-TOKEN value from your login

formdata.value: Here is a picture of the Grand Haven lighthouse

request.description: Uploads a new file (or creates a virtual/collection asset) and sets its initial metadata.

query.description: The source image TO copy. This is the assetPath value
The destination path of the file plus the name

request.description: Creates a duplicate of an asset at a new location, specified via `source` and `target`.

header.description: Get the token from the login values returned

request.description: Locks the asset at {{id}} for editing, preventing other users from checking in changes until it's checked back in or the checkout is undone.
Releases a checkout on {{id}} without saving any changes, unlocking the asset again.

query.description: Required. The folderPath or assetPath to asset to be moved or renamed

Required. The folderPath or assetPath to which the folder or asset should be moved or renamed. If the parent folder is the same as in the source path, the asset will be renamed, otherwise it will be moved.
Optional. Policy used when destination folder already exists. Options are: AUTO_RENAME, MERGE, THROW_EXCEPTION. if Omitted AUTO_RENAME will be be used.
Optional. Policy used when destination asset already exists. Options: AUTO_RENAME, OVERWRITE, OVERWRITE_IF_NEWER, REMOVE_SOURCE, THROW_EXCEPTION, DO_NOTHING. if Omitted AUTO_RENAME will be be used.
Optional. When specified, only source assets that match this query will be moved.
Optional. True/False. When set to true will move all files from source subfolders to directly below the target folder. This will 'flatten' any subfolder structure.
Optional. True/False. When true, the process will run asynchronous in the background. The call will return immediate with the processId.

request.description: Moves and/or renames an asset from `source` to `target`. `folderReplacePolicy`/`fileReplacePolicy` control conflict handling, and `async` allows the operation to run in the background for large moves.

query.description: Optional. When true, the process will run asynchronous in the background. The call will return immediate with the processId.

request.description: Deletes assets matching a query (`q`), a list of ids (`ids`), or everything under a folder (`folderPath`).

header.description: Use the X-CSRF-TOKEN value from your login

query.description: A query matching the assets that should be updated, see the query syntax guide for details.

Optional. A JSON encoded object with properties that match Assets Server metadata field names. This metadata will be set on the asset in Assets Server.

Optional. You can also use parameters matching Assets Server field names.
Any parameter matching an Assets Server metadata field name will be used as metadata. This metadata will be set on the asset in Assets Server.
Optional. When true, the process will run asynchronous in the background. The call will return immediate with the processId.
Optional. Default true. Prevents parsing "+" and "-" when set to false.

request.description: Updates metadata on multiple assets at once, targeted either by a search query (`q`) or by an explicit list of ids/metadata objects passed in the request body. Supports async execution and metadata append/remove semantics via `parseMetadataModifications`.

header.description: Use the X-CSRF-TOKEN value from your login

formdata.description: Required. The Assets Server id of the asset to be updated.
Optional. If not included only metadata is updated
Optional. JSON Encoded object. You can also use parameters matching Assets Server field names.
Comma-delimited list of metadata fields to return in hits. It is good practice to always specify just the metadata fields that you need.

Optional. When specified, the service will send a 301 redirect to this URL when it completes successfully. If you place '${id}' in the URL, it will be replaced with the Assets Server asset id of the updated asset.

request.description: Updates metadata on a single existing asset (and checks it in if it was checked out). Behaves like Create, but targets an existing asset via `id`.

header.description: Use the X-CSRF-TOKEN value from your login

query.description: Required. assetID
Optional. First hit to be returned. Starting at 0 for the first hit. Used to skip hits to return 'paged' results. Default is 0.
Number of hits to return.
Optional. Default is 0. Can be customized.
Optional. Default is empty. Returns all actions.

request.description: Retrieves the version and change history for an asset — uploads, metadata edits, moves, and more — with pagination via `start`/`num` and detail controlled by `detailLevel`/`actions`.

header.description: Use the X-CSRF-TOKEN value from your login

query.description: Required. ID of the asset which needs to be reverted.
Required. Version number of the version to which the asset needs to be reverted.

request.description: Promotes a previous version of an asset (given by `version`) back to being the current version of {{id}}.

header.description: Use the X-CSRF-TOKEN value from your login

query.description: Comma-separated list of asset ids for the ZIP file. Exactly one of assetIds, q, or folderPath must be specified.
File type to include: original or preview. Exactly one of downloadKind, downloadPresetId, or previewFormat must be specified.

request.description: Downloads multiple assets as a single ZIP archive. Select assets via `assetIds`, a search query (`q`), or `folderPath` (exactly one required), and choose the file type via `downloadKind` (original/preview), `downloadPresetId`, or `previewFormat` (exactly one required). The `archive.zip` segment in the path is just the output filename and can be renamed freely.

query.description: value

request.description: Records a usage event (e.g. a view or download) against `assetID`, tagged with an `action` label, for usage reporting.

item.description: Core lifecycle operations on individual or bulk assets: create, copy, checkout/undo checkout, move/rename, remove, bulk and single metadata updates, version history, and usage logging.

item.name: Relations

query.description: Values include: 'variation'

request.description: Creates a relationship between two assets (`target1Id` and `target2Id`) of a given `relationType` — e.g. linking an InDesign layout to an image it uses.

query.description: Required. Comma-delimited list of relation ids. A comma-delimited list of relation ids to be removed. To find the relation ids, use a relation search.

request.description: Removes one or more existing asset relations by their relation ids.

item.description: Linking and unlinking relationships between two assets (e.g. an InDesign layout and its linked images).

item.name: Auth Keys (Sharelinks)

query.description: Required - Subject for the AuthKey
Required - Expiry Date (for Format see Date Patterns here: https://helpcenter.woodwing.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041854192
Comma delimited list of Asset IDs
Description for authKey
true/false - default false
true/false - default false
true/false - default false
true/false - default false - must be true if assetIDs value is null
optional - relevant when requestUpload is true
list, comma-separated, of containers for preview or download
Required when 'requestUpload' is true. Optional otherwise. folderPath where files will be upload.
Email address to send notifications to when upload or approval is finished. Required when requestUpload or requestApproval is true. Optional otherwise.
Client setting. Optional
Comma-delimited list of Download Preset IDs. Optional.
Shows watermarks on thumbnails and previews in Shared Links. Optional

request.description: Creates a Shared Link (authKey) for the given `assetIds`, with an expiry (`validUntil`) and access options such as `downloadOriginal`, `downloadPreview`, `requestApproval`, and `requestUpload`. Returns the authKey plus ready-to-use web/desktop/mobile client URLs.

query.description: Required. The authKey which will be updated.
Optional. Subject for the AuthKey
Optional. Expiry date, in one of the date formats supported by Assets Server, see the query syntax guide for details.
Optional. Description for authKey
Optional. true/false - default false
Optional. true/false - default false
Optional. true/false - default false
Optional. true/false - default false - must be true if assetIDs value is null
Optional - relevant when requestUpload is true
list, comma-separated, of containers for preview or download
Required when 'requestUpload' is true. Optional otherwise. folderPath where files will be upload.
Optional. Email address to send notifications to when upload or approval is finished. Required when requestUpload or requestApproval is true. Optional otherwise.
Optional. Client setting.
Optional. thumbnail, list, mason are values. Default is thumbnail
Optional. Client setting, comma-delimited list of fieldnames for showing metadata in the thumbnail view.
Optional. Client setting, comma-delimited list of fieldnames for showing metadata in the list view.
Optional. Client setting, comma-delimited list of fieldnames for showing metadata in the filmstrip view.
Optional. Client setting, thumbnail zoom level in the thumbnail view.
Optional. Client setting, thumbnail zoom level in the list view.
Optional. Client setting, thumbnail zoom level in the filmstrip view.
Optional. Comma-delimited list of Download Preset id's. Allows the downloading of renditions generated according to these Preset settings.

request.description: Updates properties (subject, expiry, description, access options) of an existing Shared Link identified by `key`. Note: assets/ids on the link cannot be changed after creation.

query.description: Required. Comma-delimited list of authKeys.

request.description: Immediately revokes one or more Shared Links, identified by `keys`, so their URLs stop working.

item.description: Creating, updating, and revoking Shared Links (authKeys) that let external users view, download, or upload assets without an Assets Server login.

item.name: Folders

query.description: The full folderPath of the folder to be created. Required. To create multiple folders repeat the 'path' parameter multiple times, each with a unique folder path

request.description: Creates a new folder at the given `path` in the Assets folder tree, using the legacy /services API.

item.description: Creating folders in the Assets folder tree via the legacy API.

item.name: Renditions

request.description: Lists the Rendition Presets available to the current user.
Lists all Rendition Presets in the system (admin-level view, not filtered by user permissions).

body.raw:
{
        "id": "",
        "name": "Jeff Gapp API Preset",
        "type": "IMAGE_PRESET",
        "creator": "Sam Spade",
        "sharedWithAuthKey": false,
        "sharedWithGroups": [
            ""
        ],
        "renditionSettings": {
            "format": "jpg",
            "scale": 50.0,
            "maxWidth": 100,
            "maxHeight": 100,
            "maxLength": -1,
            "ppi": 72,
            "ppiEnabled": true,
            "page": -1,
            "embedMetadata": true,
            "embedColorProfile": true,
            "compression": "",
            "quality": 100,
            "resizeMode": "scale",
            "watermarked": false,
            "background": "",
            "cropWidth": -1,
            "cropHeight": -1,
            "cropOffsetX": -1,
            "cropOffsetY": -1,
            "skipfront": -1
        }
    }
]

request.description: Creates a new Rendition Preset, or updates an existing one if `id` is supplied. `name` sets the preset's display name.

query.description: Required. ID of preset rendition

request.description: Deletes one or more Rendition Presets by `ids`.

item.description: Managing Rendition Presets — named sets of image/format transformation settings used to generate on-the-fly asset renditions.

item.name: Misc

request.description: Sends a notification e-mail through Assets Server's built-in mail configuration — the same mechanism used for Shared Link notifications.

query.description: Comma separated list of locales. Default is us_EN.
Optional. The date of the last requested cached messages. Use this when caching the messages in your client locally. If omitted then the messages will always be returned.
Optional. Default is web. Options are 'web' or 'acm'

request.description: Retrieves localized UI strings for a given `localeChain` (e.g. `en-US`) and `bundle`, with `ifModifiedSince` support for caching.
Retrieves details about the user authenticated in the current session (username, full name, user zone, groups, email, and granted authorities).

item.description: Supporting utility calls: sending notification e-mails and fetching localized UI strings. Also includes a 'Profile' request whose current endpoint doesn't match its name — see that request's description.

item.name: Processes

header.description: Use the X-CSRF-TOKEN value from your login

query.description: Required. ID of the process to query, as returned when the process was started.

request.description: Returns the current status of a long-running server process (e.g. bulk import, stats cleanup) by its `processId`. A process can only be viewed by a superuser or by the user who started it; finished processes remain queryable for a limited time.

header.description: Use the X-CSRF-TOKEN value from your login

query.description: Required. ID of the process to cancel.

request.description: Cancels a running server process by its `processId`. Only works if the process supports cancellation (`cancelable: true` in its status).

item.description: Track and cancel long-running server processes (e.g. bulk import, stats cleanup). Only a superuser or the user who started a process can view or cancel it.
Calls against the original (legacy) Assets Server /services API — the JSON-RPC-style REST API available since Assets 6.

item.name: REST API - Management Console
Working with assets

request.description: Creates a new asset via the REST API by posting a JSON body with metadata and file details.

Uploads or replaces the original file content for asset {{id}} using a signed `requestSecret` (obtained from a prior create/upload-authorization call). `createVersion=true` stores the upload as a new version rather than overwriting the current one.

query.description: The search query.
The offset from the first result you want to fetch. Default is 0.
The maximum amount of hits to be returned. Default is 10.

request.description: REST equivalent of the legacy Search call — full-text/metadata search (here for `apollo`) with `from`/`size` pagination, returning JSON asset objects.

query.description: Number of minutes the URL is valid for. -1 provides a URL that doesn't expire.
Required. Values are jpg, png, tiff

Scales the width and height of the rendition relative to the original size. The scale is in percentages from 1 to 100. If used in combination with maxWidth or maxHeight the image will be scaled with a maximum size.

Sets the maximum width of the preview. The aspect ratio will always remain the same. If the original asset fits within the supplied dimensions, the original width and height will be used for generating the rendition.

Sets the maximum height of the preview. The aspect ratio will always remain the same. If the original asset fits within the supplied dimensions, the original width and height will be used for generating the rendition.
The pixels per inch / dots per inch can be set to any desired value. When not set, the original PPI / DPI value of the asset will be used.

When set to true, the metadata embedded in the original asset will be embedded in the generated rendition. Note that for certain file types the embedding of metadata is not supported, for example for PNG files. By default the original metadata is not embedded.
When set to true, the color profile that is used to generate the rendition is included.
TIFF Compression type, applicable when format=’tif’. Allowed values: uncompressed, packBits, LZW, ZIP and JPEG.
JPEG compression, applicable when format=’jpg’. The quality is defined a value from 1 to 100.
When set to true, a watermark is applied to the rendition. Note: a watermark needs to be configured in the server configuration.
Sets the background color (hex color code) for transparent images. If not specified, the output of PNG and TIFF images will be transparent.
Sets the width (in pixels) to which the image should be cropped.
Sets the height (in pixels) to which the image should be cropped.
Sets the crop x offset in pixels (from the top left corner).
Sets the crop y offset in pixels (from the top left corner).

request.description: Generates a short-lived, pre-authenticated URL (valid for `validFor` seconds, here 600) for downloading a specific image rendition (`format=jpg`) of asset(s) {{ids}}, without needing to pass session credentials — useful for embedding in e-mails or external apps.

query.description: Number of minutes the URL is valid for. -1 provides a URL that doesn't expire.

request.description: Generates a short-lived, pre-authenticated URL (valid for `validFor` seconds) for downloading the original file of asset(s) {{ids}} without session credentials.

query.description: Number of minutes the URL is valid for. -1 provides a URL that doesn't expire.

request.description: Generates a short-lived, pre-authenticated URL (valid for `validFor` seconds) for downloading the preview rendition of asset(s) {{ids}} without session credentials.

query.description: Number of minutes the URL is valid for. -1 provides a URL that doesn't expire.

request.description: Generates a short-lived, pre-authenticated URL (valid for `validFor` seconds) for downloading the thumbnail of asset(s) {{ids}} without session credentials.
Retrieves the full metadata record for a single asset by {{id}}.
Downloads the original file for asset {{id}} directly, using the current session's auth.
Uploads file content for asset {{id}} as multipart form data, replacing the current original.
Uploads file content for asset {{id}} as a raw PUT body, replacing the current original.
Downloads the preview rendition for asset {{id}} directly, using the current session's auth.
Downloads the thumbnail image for asset {{id}} directly, using the current session's auth.

item.description: Creating, uploading, downloading, and retrieving individual assets and their derived renditions (original, preview, thumbnail) via the REST API, including short-lived signed URLs for unauthenticated access.

item.name: Create Sharelink

request.description: REST equivalent of the legacy Create Auth Key call — creates a Shared Link for external access to one or more assets via the REST API.

item.description: REST equivalent of the legacy Auth Keys (Sharelinks) folder — creates Shared Links for external access to assets.

item.name: Folder

request.description: Creates a new folder via the REST API, optionally with metadata attached at creation time.

query.description: Path to the folder you want

request.description: Retrieves a folder's details using its full path (e.g. `/Demo Zone/Postman`) instead of its id.

query.description: Folder identifier (generally top level)
The offset from the first result you want to fetch. Default value : 0
The maximum amount of hits to be returned. Default value : 10

Metadata fields to return for folders. Specify ‘all’ to return all available metadata. Specify ‘none’ to return no metadata.

Default value : all

request.description: Lists the subfolders directly under {{folder_id}}, with pagination (`from`/`size`) and optional metadata fields to return (here `tags`).

query.description: Folder identifier (generally top level)
The offset from the first result you want to fetch. Default value : 0
The maximum amount of hits to be returned. Default value : 10

Metadata fields to return for folders. Specify ‘all’ to return all available metadata. Specify ‘none’ to return no metadata.

Default value : all

request.description: Searches for folders by name or metadata (here `name:postman`), with pagination and optional metadata fields to return.
Retrieves a single folder's details by {{folder_id}}.
Updates the metadata of the folder at {{folder_id}}.
Deletes the folder at {{folder_id}}.

item.description: Full CRUD for folders (create, read by path/id, search, update metadata, delete) via the REST API.
Calls against the newer, purely RESTful Assets API (JSON in/out, standard HTTP verbs), documented in the Management Console's API reference.

item.name: External processing

request.description: Registers a new External Processing engine — an external service Assets Server can call out to for asset processing (e.g. AI-based auto-tagging, face/object recognition, or transcoding).
Lists all registered External Processing engines.
Retrieves the configuration of a single External Processing engine by {{external_engine_id}}.
Updates the configuration of an existing External Processing engine identified by {{external_engine_id}}.
Deletes a single External Processing engine, identified by {{external_engine_id}}.
Deletes all registered External Processing engines at once, via the literal `all` path segment — distinct from deleting a single engine by id.

item.description: Registers and manages External Processing engines — external services (e.g. AI-based tagging or transcoding) that Assets Server can call out to for asset processing. Marked 'not in final form' in the source README, so treat as subject to change.

item.name: Metrics

request.description: Returns server-level usage metrics for Assets Server. Unauthenticated — no session cookie or CSRF token required.

item.description: Server-level usage metrics for Assets Server.
